danger_signals.visual_form: Generic enemy sprites spawning from stage edges
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinctive boss sprite with unique design
danger_signals.meaning: Major danger spike; requires blocking and tactical engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual animation changes after successful block
danger_signals.meaning: Danger is temporarily reduced; counter-attack window open

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite damage state or health bar reduction
Screen-wide attack animation with visual effect
danger_signals.meaning: High-risk maneuver; player health loss occurring

safety_signals.visual_form: Character enters guard stance with invincibility frames
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability window; safe to counter-attack

safety_signals.visual_form: Scene transition to new arena/boss stage
safety_signals.meaning: Previous threats cleared; advancing through narrative

safety_signals.visual_form: Equipment and armor visual upgrades on character sprite

faction_indicators.color_identity: Arthur: chain mail/leather transitioning to silver and gold armor; Lancelot: blue tabard with yellow cross, plate armor; Perceval: light armor with green pants transitioning to heavier armor
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Excalibur (Arthur's sword), Sabre (Lancelot's weapon), Battle-axe (Perceval's weapon)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: British medieval setting; horseback combat capability; castle/kingdom stages

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Generic enemies with standard medieval combat appearance; boss characters with distinctive individual designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Opposed to player progression; staged in various locations requiring conquest

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Unique boss sprite design representing tyrannical ruler
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: End-stage encounter; rules the corrupted kingdom requiring liberation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy-filled stages; medieval battleground environments; hostile NPCs throughout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Generic enemies actively attack player; bosses present blocking-dependent combat mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character armor and weapon visual upgrades; advancing through sequential stages; mounting horses for traversal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies scaled to player level; new weapon/armor unlocks at progression gates; boss vulnerability patterns emerge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three distinct playable knight characters representing different approaches (balanced/fast/strong); collaborative multiplayer option
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple character selection reflects diverse approaches to liberation; cooperative gameplay enables shared victory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss character animations showing attack patterns; blocking stance visuals; invincibility frames after successful blocks
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat requires blocking timing rather than pure offense; bosses vulnerable only after successful defensive maneuver

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Horse sprites appearing in specific stage sections; character mounted on horse sprite
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ability to stomp enemies with horse; some bosses and enemies also mounted; horseback mechanics provide tactical variety

environmental_storytelling.example: Arthur begins in chain mail/leather, upgrades to increasingly ornate silver and gold armor, visualizing his journey from trained warrior to king-in-waiting

Lancelot's slender sprite and blue tabard suggest agility/nobility; Perceval's broad-shouldered build and bare chest armor convey raw strength; Arthur's balanced appearance reflects well-rounded capability
Excalibur (sacred legendary sword), Sabre (traveler's blade suggesting wandering), Battle-axe (craftsman's tool repurposed as weapon) each communicate character background and role
Sequential stages represent conquering different regions of Britain; final confrontation with Garibaldi signifies restoring rightful rule
Horseback combat sections provide visual spectacle and mechanical advantage, emphasizing player progression and knight fantasy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Spawn from stage edges and advance toward player
Attack player character; can be blocked and defeated
Attack with specific patterns; cannot be damaged effectively
Temporarily vulnerable; player gains invincibility window to counter-attack
Can ride horses; adds tactical complexity to boss encounters
Gains few seconds of invincibility frames; guard drops if not struck during block window
Clears most screen enemies at health cost; visual indicator of high-risk tactic
Performs jumping kick special attack; fastest character movement speed
Can dash-cancel into Giant Swing attack; slowest character but highest damage output
Most devastating when on horseback; visual indication of superior mounted combat capability

notes: Knights of the Round communicates world-state primarily through character visual progression (armor/weapon upgrades), enemy spawning patterns, and combat mechanic disclosure (blocking vulnerability windows). The Arthurian legend setting provides narrative context but visual communication focuses on combat readiness rather than political faction differentiation. The three playable knights visually represent different approaches to the same quest (speed vs. power vs. balance), suggesting a unified but multi-faceted resistance against Garibaldi's rule. Environmental storytelling relies on character design and equipment upgrades rather than environmental props or NPC behavior—typical of beat 'em up genre conventions. The blocking mechanic and boss vulnerability patterns are communicated through animation states rather than UI elements, requiring player observation and learning.
